info.name: Ant Media Server REST API Reference
Adds a third party RTMP end point to the stream

params.name: resolutionHeight
params.description: Resolution height of the broadcast that is wanted to send to the RTMP endpoint.

items.docs: It supports adding after broadcast is started. Resolution can be specified to send a specific adaptive resolution. If an URL is already added to a stream, trying to add the same RTMP URL will return false.

info.name: Remove third-party RTMP end point from the stream

params.name: endpointServiceId
params.description: RTMP url of the endpoint that will be stopped.

params.name: resolutionHeight
params.description: Resolution specifier if endpoint has been added with resolution. Only applicable if user added RTMP endpoint with a resolution speficier. Otherwise won't work and won't remove the endpoint.

items.docs: For the stream that is broadcasting, it will stop immediately.

info.name: addID3Data

items.docs: Add ID3 data to HLS stream at the moment

info.name: addSEIData

items.docs: Add SEI data to HLS stream at the moment

info.name: Add a subtrack to a main track (broadcast)

items.docs: Adds a subtrack to a main track (broadcast).

info.name: Delete a subtrack from a main track (broadcast)

items.docs: Deletes a subtrack from a main track (broadcast).

info.name: Add Subscriber to the requested stream

items.docs: Adds a subscriber to the requested stream. If the subscriber's type is 'publish', they can also play the stream, which is critical in conferencing. If the subscriber's type is 'play', they can only play the stream. If 'b32Secret' is not set, it will default to the AppSettings. The length of 'b32Secret' should be a multiple of 8 and use base32 characters A?Z, 2?7.

info.name: Removes all subscribers related to the requested stream

items.docs: Deletes all subscriber data associated with the specified stream including ConnectionEvents.

info.name: Block specific subscriber

params.name: type
params.description: block type it can be 'publish', 'play' or 'publish_play'

items.docs: Blocks a specific subscriber, enhancing security especially when used with TOTP streaming. The subscriber is blocked for a specified number of seconds from the moment this method is called.

info.name: createBroadcast

params.name: autoStart
params.description: Only effective if stream is IP Camera or Stream Source. If it's true, it starts automatically pulling stream. Its value is false by default

items.docs: Creates a Broadcast, IP Camera or Stream Source and returns the full broadcast object with rtmp address and other information. The different between Broadcast and IP Camera or Stream Source is that Broadcast is ingested by Ant Media ServerIP Camera or Stream Source is pulled by Ant Media Server

info.name: getBroadcast

items.docs: Get broadcast object

info.name: updateBroadcast

items.docs: Updates the Broadcast objects fields if it's not null. The updated fields are as follows: name, description, userName, password, IP address, streamUrl of the broadcast. It also updates the social endpoints

info.name: Delete broadcast from data store and stop if it's broadcasting

items.docs: Delete broadcast from data store and stop if it's broadcasting

info.name: deleteBroadcastsBulk

items.docs: Delete multiple broadcasts from data store and stop if they are broadcasting

info.name: Delete specific subscriber from data store

items.docs: Deletes a specific subscriber from the data store for the selected stream.

info.name: Set stream specific recording setting

params.name: recording-status
params.description: Change recording status. If true, starts recording. If false stop recording

params.name: recordType
params.description: Record type: 'mp4' or 'webm'. It's optional parameter.

params.name: resolutionHeight
params.description: Resolution height of the broadcast that is wanted to record.

items.docs: This setting overrides the general Mp4 and WebM Muxing Setting for a specific stream.

info.name: Return the active live streams

items.docs: Retrieves the currently active live streams.

info.name: getBroadcastList

params.name: offset
params.description: This is the offset of the list, it is useful for pagination. If you want to use sort mechanism, we recommend using Mongo DB.

params.name: size
params.description: Number of items that will be fetched. If there is not enough item in the datastore, returned list size may less then this value

params.name: type_by
params.description: Type of the stream. Possible values are "liveStream", "ipCamera", "streamSource", "VoD"

params.name: sort_by
params.description: Field to sort. Possible values are "name", "date", "status"

params.name: search
params.description: Search parameter, returns specific items that contains search string

items.docs: Gets the broadcast list from database. It returns max 50 items at a time

info.name: Get the broadcast live statistics

items.docs: Retrieves live statistics of the broadcast, including total RTMP watcher count, total HLS watcher count, and total WebRTC watcher count.

info.name: Get total broadcast live statistics

items.docs: Retrieves total live statistics of the broadcast, including total HLS watcher count and total WebRTC watcher count.

info.name: Get IP Camera Error after connection failure

params.name: streamId
params.description: StreamId of the IP Camera Streaming.

items.docs: Checks for an error after a connection failure with an IP camera. Returning true indicates an error; false indicates no error.

info.name: Retrieve all subscriber statistics of the requested stream. Deprecated

params.name: offset
params.description: the starting point of the list

params.name: subscriberId
params.description: subscriberId to filter the connections events

items.docs: Fetches comprehensive statistics for all subscribers of the specified stream.

info.name: Retrieve detected objects from the stream

params.name: size
params.description: total size of the return list

items.docs: Fetches detected objects from the stream, using specified offset and size parameters.

params.name: url
params.description: Url of the stream that its duration will be returned

items.docs: Gets the durations of the stream url in milliseconds

info.name: getJwtTokenV2

params.name: expireDate
params.description: The expire time of the token. It's in unix timestamp seconds.

params.name: type
params.description: Type of the JWT token. It may be play or publish

params.name: roomId
params.description: Room Id that token belongs to. It's not mandatory

items.docs: Generates JWT token for specified stream. It's not required to let the server generate JWT. Generally JWT tokens should be generated on the client side.

info.name: Get total number of detected objects

items.docs: Retrieves the total count of objects detected.

params.name: id
params.description: The id of the IP Camera

items.docs: Retrieves the profile list for an ONVIF IP camera.

info.name: Get RTMP to WebRTC Path Stats

items.docs: Retrieves general statistics for the RTMP to WebRTC path.

info.name: Get stream information

items.docs: Returns the stream information including width, height, bitrates, and video codec.

info.name: getTOTP

params.name: id
params.description: The id of the stream that TOTP will be generated

params.name: sid
params.description: The id of the subscriber that TOTP will be generated

params.name: type
params.description: The type of token. It's being used if subscriber is not in the database. It can be publish, play

items.docs: Return TOTP for the subscriberId, streamId, type. This is a helper method. You can generate TOTP on your end.If subscriberId is not in the database, it generates TOTP from the secret in the AppSettings. Secret code is for the subscriberId not in the database secretCode = Base32.encodeAsString({secretFromSettings(publishsecret or playsecret according to the type)} + {subscriberId} + {streamId} + {type(publish or play)} + {Number of X to have the length multiple of 8}'+' means concatenating the st

info.name: getTokenV2

params.name: expireDate
params.description: The expire time of the token. It's in unix timestamp seconds

params.name: type
params.description: Type of the token. It may be play or publish

params.name: roomId
params.description: Room Id that token belongs to. It's not mandatory

items.docs: Generates random one-time token for specified stream

info.name: Retrieve all subscriber statistics of the requested stream. Deprecated use connection-events method.

params.name: offset
params.description: the starting point of the list

items.docs: Fetches comprehensive statistics for all subscribers of the specified stream. Deprecated use connection-events method. This method is kept for backward compatibility and getting old records. New records saved and retrieved with connection-events method because there is a schema design causes performance issues

info.name: Get all subscribers of the requested stream

params.name: offset
params.description: the starting point of the list

items.docs: It does not return subscriber-stats. Please use subscriber-stats method

info.name: Get all tokens of requested stream

params.name: offset
params.description: the starting point of the list

items.docs: Get all tokens of requested stream

info.name: Move IP Camera

params.name: id
params.description: The id of the IP Camera

params.name: valueX
params.description: Movement in X direction. If not specified, it's assumed to be zero. Valid ranges between -1.0f and 1.0f for all movements

params.name: valueY
params.description: Movement in Y direction. If not specified, it's assumed to be zero. Valid ranges between -1.0f and 1.0f for all movements

params.name: valueZ
params.description: Movement in Zoom. If not specified, it's assumed to be zero. Valid ranges for relative and continous move is between -1.0f and 1.0f. For absolute move between 0.0f and 1.0f

params.name: movement
params.description: Movement type. It can be absolute, relative or continuous. If not specified, it's relative

items.docs: Supports continuous, relative, and absolute movement. By default, it's a relative move. Movement parameters should be provided according to the movement type. Generally, the following values are used: For Absolute move, value X and value Y are between -1.0f and 1.0f. Zoom value is between 0.0f and 1.0f. For Relative move, value X, value Y, and Zoom Value are between -1.0f and 1.0f. For Continuous move, value X, value Y, and Zoom Value are between -1.0f and 1.0f.

info.name: Specify the next playlist item to play by index

params.name: id
params.description: The id of the playlist stream.

params.name: index
params.description: The next item to play. If it's not specified or it's -1, it plays next item. If it's number, it skips that item in the playlist to play. The first item index is 0.

items.docs: Sets the next playlist item to be played, based on its index. This method is applicable only to playlists.
